hero.description1: Living education project for preschool and primary school in Sant Esteve de Palautordera, in the heart of Montseny.
hero.description2: Here you can consult our pedagogical project, our team, the spaces, our references, and do not hesitate to contact us for a visit.

second.title: Born from
second.text1: La Llavor was born from an unexpected need, but also from a very deep conviction: that childhood deserves to grow in spaces where it is met with respect, trust, and love for its own rhythm.
second.text2: In August 2025, a group of families from Baix Montseny unexpectedly found themselves without an educational project for their children. Faced with this situation, they decided to come together and begin looking for alternatives aligned with their view of childhood and with the need for a more conscious and respectful educational accompaniment.
second.text3: Along this path, support arrived from El Planter, the secondary project that accompanies teenagers through a living education approach. Together with an initial team of educators, the El Planter team and the families began to imagine and build a shared dream for those of us working in alternative education: to offer families the possibility that their children can grow and move through preschool, primary and secondary stages within the same coherent and respectful educational ecosystem, in one physical space in Baix Montseny, in contact with nature and the territory.
second.text4: This is how La Llavor begins to take shape.
second.readMoreTitle: This is how La Llavor begins to take shape.
second.readMoreIntro: During this process, the project was also searching for its name, the word that could represent what was being born. Many proposals emerged, speaking of nature, growth and life: El Brot, L’Avet, La Fageda, L’Arrel, La Llavor, La Llavor de Colors, L’Arbre de Colors, El Rebrot, El Cabirol, L’Esquirol, La Guineu, El Pinyol, L’Esqueix, La Malva, L’Espurna… Among all of them, La Llavor ultimately resonated strongly, because it symbolizes beginnings, potential, and trust in all that can grow if it is cared for with time, respect, and dedication.
second.readMoreP1: The beginnings were intense: long conversations, trials, doubts, moments of uncertainty, and also a great deal of hope. As happens with projects that are truly born from something real, the path was not linear. The team kept transforming, searching for the right shape so this dream could take root with coherence and solidity.
second.readMoreP2: In this process, an important step also takes place: El Planter decides to move its project and settle in the farmhouse we now share in the heart of Baix Montseny, making it possible for both projects to live together within the same educational ecosystem. Childhood and adolescence thus share a living space where different stages of life can grow, observe one another, and inspire each other.
second.readMoreP3: Some days, the space also receives visits from La Selva, an educational accompaniment proposal in the forest that is also part of the cooperative currently supporting El Planter. Once a week, its children and educators visit the farmhouse and share moments of life and learning with the community.
second.readMoreP4: Their presence enriches the educational ecosystem that inhabits this corner of Baix Montseny, bringing a deep perspective of connection with the forest and with nature. Despite this closeness and collaboration, each project maintains its own identity, its team, and its own path.
second.readMoreP5: This respectful coexistence between different but kindred projects reminds us that educating also means creating community, weaving bonds, and opening spaces where diverse ways of accompanying childhood can converse and inspire one another.
second.readMoreP6: At the end of January 2026, the project takes an important turn. Tamara Depares assumes direction and coordination, consolidates the educational team, and gives structure to the rhythm of daily life. From that point on, the project begins to gain strength, clarity, and rootedness.
second.readMoreP7: What began as a need transforms into a community.
second.readMoreP8: Today La Llavor is a living space in Baix Montseny where children can explore, investigate, play, make mistakes, create, and build their own learning in contact with nature and with the rhythm of real life. A place where curiosity is welcome, where questions have value, and where each child is accompanied with respect for their process.
second.readMoreP9: A place where the time of childhood is cared for with presence, awareness, and love.
second.readMoreP10: If you want to know more deeply the educational perspective that sustains this path, you can consult our full pedagogical project here.
second.readMorePdfLink: To read the full pedagogical project in pdf, click here.

team.title: The La Llavor team
team.description: As a team, we always keep very present in our accompaniment the child we once were.

profile.contactNote: If you would like to know more about {{name}}'s way of accompanying and our pedagogy, please feel free to contact us.
profile.joseContactNote: If you would like to know more about José's accompaniment and our pedagogy, please feel free to contact us.
profile.cvLink: To view the full CV, click here

tamara.bio:
Graduate in Philosophy, guide in living education and conscious parenting processes, with more than 10 years of experience accompanying children, families, and educational teams.
Currently leads the pedagogical project of La Llavor, from a perspective that integrates living education, body, emotion, and systemic awareness.

noel.bio: Child guide, psycho-corporal integration therapist, and Aucouturier preventive psychomotricist.

jose.bio:
Guide in living education projects and emotional educator in non-formal education.

I really enjoy sharing experiences with people of all ages and being able to accompany both individual and group processes. All of this, combined with a strong desire to learn, research, and experiment, also helps me move forward in my own life process.

amanda.bio: Artist and creator who combines ancestral techniques with natural fabrics using upcycling. Transforming materials with hands, mind, and heart.

sarai.bio: Willing to fill the world with colors since childhood, her love for art led her to become an illustrator and art historian. What she values most about her profession is sharing with others the creative and channeling possibilities of art.

contact.title: We'd like to hear from you.
contact.description: Send a message and we'll respond as soon as possible.

info.footer: We are open to enquiries, visits and collaborations.

footer.text1: Morning arrives unannounced — just a slow wash of gold across the wooden floor, warming stones that have endured the cold all night.
footer.text2: The kitchen smells of coffee and pine. An open window lets mountain air settle between the pages of an unread book.
footer.text3: Some mornings the fog is so thick you forget there's a valley below. You stand at the door and breathe, and that's enough.
footer.text4: Each evening the mountains rehearse their disappearance — edges soften, colors drain, until only silhouettes remain against the last light.
footer.text5: The wood stove ticks and sighs. Outside, stars appear one by one, as if someone remembered them into existence.
footer.text6: This is the place where time goes to rest. Not lost — simply unhurried, settling like dust in a late afternoon sunbeam.

content.intro: At La Llavor, we respect your privacy and are committed to protecting the personal data you provide. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use and protect your information when you visit our website or interact with us.
content.info2: When you use our contact form, we collect your name, email and message. This information is sent directly to our team via Formspree, a third-party service.
content.cookiesText: We use cookies to improve your experience on our website. You can manage your cookie preferences through our cookie banner.
content.usage: We use the collected information to:
content.usage1: Respond to your inquiries and messages.
content.sharing: We do not sell, rent or share your personal data with third parties, except when necessary to provide our services (such as Formspree for the contact form) or when required by law.
content.security: We take reasonable measures to protect your personal data against loss, misuse or unauthorized access. However, no method of internet transmission is 100% secure.
content.rights: You have the right to access, correct, delete or limit the use of your personal data. To exercise these rights, contact us through our contact form.
content.changes: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page.
content.contact: If you have questions about this privacy policy, contact us at

banner.description: We use essential cookies for the site to work and, if you accept them, Google Analytics cookies to understand how the website is used.

content.intro: This cookie policy explains how we use cookies and similar technologies on our website. By continuing to browse our site, you agree to the use of cookies in accordance with this policy.
content.what: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device when you visit a website. They allow us to remember your preferences, improve your browsing experience, and analyze site usage.
content.types: We use the following types of cookies:
content.essentialDesc: These cookies are necessary for the basic functioning of the website, such as remembering your acceptance of the cookie banner and maintaining site functionality.
content.analyticsDesc: We use analytics cookies to collect information about how visitors use our website, such as pages visited and time spent. This information helps us improve the site.
content.functionalDesc: These cookies allow the site to remember choices you make (such as your preferred language) and provide enhanced and more personal features.
content.manage: You can manage your cookie preferences through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies. However, please note that disabling certain cookies may affect site functionality.
content.contact: If you have questions about our cookie policy, you can contact us through our contact form.
